title: InventoryItem
description: The canonical shape of an InventoryItem record — docs/50-PHASE-3-TECHNICAL-PLAN.md §10's design, Phase 3/WHIMS Batch WPI-7 (docs/50 §19), governed by docs/53-PHASE-3-IMPLEMENTATION-RULES.md. A stock-keeping record — never patient-facing, doctor/staff-facing only, exposed through the Doctor Dashboard's `inventory` capability. `quantity_on_hand` is a derived/cached value, recomputed from the append-only `InventoryTransaction` ledger (inventory-transaction.schema.json) every time a transaction is recorded — it is never the sole source of truth and is never accepted directly from a create/update request; a new item always starts at 0 and its real starting stock is set by recording an initial `restock`/`adjustment` transaction against it, the same 'never accept a derived field from input' discipline appointment.schema.json's `specialty_slug` already establishes. See inventory-item.md for the full disclosure, including why `quantity_on_hand` is never directly writable and the disclosed, additive `created_by`/`created_at` provenance fields.

inventory_item_id.description: This row's own identity column (UUID), server-generated. Never staff-supplied.

name.description: The stock-keeping unit's human-readable name (e.g. a remedy/product name). Staff-supplied at creation, immutable thereafter in this batch's scope.

sku.description: The stock-keeping unit's own identifying code. Staff-supplied at creation. No uniqueness constraint is enforced by this batch — docs/50 §10 does not name one, and inventing one would be undocumented scope beyond this entity's designed shape.

unit.description: The unit this item is counted in (e.g. 'tablets', 'ml', 'bottles'). Staff-supplied at creation, free text — docs/50 §10 does not name a fixed enum for this field.

quantity_on_hand.description: A derived/cached running total, recomputed in full from the `InventoryTransaction` ledger's own `change_qty` rows every time a transaction is recorded against this item (docs/50 §10, docs/54 §13's recovery-strategy discipline) — never the sole source of truth, and always reconstructable by replaying the ledger. Always 0 at creation; never accepted from a create/update request afterward. Every write to this field is wrapped in `LockService` (apps-script/InventoryTransaction.gs's `foundationRecordInventoryTransaction_()`) per docs/54 §7/§19's required mitigation.

reorder_threshold.description: The stock level at or below which an `inventory_low_stock` Notification (notification.schema.json) is produced (docs/50 §10, docs/54 §19). Staff-supplied at creation, editable via the retire/threshold-update path this batch adds.

specialty_scope.description: Optional specialty scoping (ADR-018, docs/50 §6.2) — empty-string sentinel means visible regardless of specialty (today's actual, only-seeded-specialty behavior), mirroring every other entity's own optional `specialty_scope`/nullable-field convention. When non-empty, must reference a real Specialty Registry entry (`SpecialtyRegistry.gs`).

status.description: This item's current lifecycle state (docs/50 §10). Always 'active' at creation. Transitions to 'retired' one-way, never reverted — a retired item no longer appears in the Doctor Dashboard's inventory view and can no longer receive a new transaction, mirroring every other Foundation-family lifecycle entity's 'no update, no delete, one-way transition' discipline.

created_by.description: The doctor/staff identifier who created this item (a disclosed, additive provenance field beyond docs/50 §10's literal attribute list — the same category of implementation-time field-level decision appointment.schema.json's own `created_by` already made). Never patient-supplied.

created_at.description: Server-set at creation (foundationNowIso_()), ISO 8601 UTC date-time. Never staff-supplied.
